|
---
**🤔 DID YOU KNOW?** Which U.S. states are **actually farther south than Mexico**? It’s not a trick—parts of Arizona, New Mexico, Texas, Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, Georgia, Florida, Hawaii, and even South Carolina all dip below Mexico’s northernmost point. So “south of the border” might not mean what you think!

name: Team TFVC
subject: TFVC Tuesday: Texas Leads the Way in Holding Big Pharma Accountable
body:
|
---
| | | [ ](https://xdzgmz.clicks.mlsend.com/ty/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyMDEyNjAsXCJsXCI6MTUxMTE1MDEyNDg3Nzc2MTc5LFwiclwiOjE1MTExNTA1MjkzNTU0NjEyMn0iLCJzIjoiMDlhYjg3NDcwMzM1ZDg0YSJ9)
---
|
# Texas Leads the Way in Holding Big Pharma Accountable
---
| |
---
| Last week, HB 3441, a novel bill by freshman Representative Shelley Luther, was heard in the House Committee on Judiciary and Civil Jurisprudence. The premise is simple: **if vaccine manufacturers choose to advertise their products in Texas, they must take on financial responsibility for those who are injured by these products.** If passed, this legislation would be the first step in correcting the huge mistake made by our US Congress in 1986 when the National Childhood Vaccine Injury Act was signed into law, giving vaccine manufacturers complete immunity from liability. Since the NCVIA went into effect, we have witnessed **an explosion in the routine childhood vaccine schedule** , along with an alarming increase in childhood chronic diseases and disabilities. Rep. Luther put it brilliantly: “the system we have today is the result of our decision to **test a thesis that was made in 1986** ...and the thesis ultimately was that we could improve health outcomes by eliminating accountability for those outcomes...we are getting exactly what our system incentivizes, which is efficiency and speed of development.” HB 3441 was left pending in Committee. **Join TFVC in asking that the Committee pass this favorably and onto the full House floor for a vote!**
---
|

clean_body: | | | | | Texas Leads the Way in Holding Big Pharma Accountable | | | Last week, HB 3441, a novel bill by freshman Representative Shelley Luther, was heard in the House Committee on Judiciary and Civil Jurisprudence. The premise is simple: if vaccine manufacturers choose to advertise their products in Texas, they must take on financial responsibility for those who are injured by these products. If passed, this legislation would be the first step in correcting the huge mistake made by our US Congress in 1986 when the National Childhood Vaccine Injury Act was signed into law, giving vaccine manufacturers complete immunity from liability. Since the NCVIA went into effect, we have witnessed an explosion in the routine childhood vaccine schedule , along with an alarming increase in childhood chronic diseases and disabilities. Rep. Luther put it brilliantly: “the system we have today is the result of our decision to test a thesis that was made in 1986 ...and the thesis ultimately was that we could improve health outcomes by eliminating accountability for those outcomes...we are getting exactly what our system incentivizes, which is efficiency and speed of development.” HB 3441 was left pending in Committee. Join TFVC in asking that the Committee pass this favorably and onto the full House floor for a vote! | | | mailto:Jeff.Leach@house.texas.gov,Ann.Johnson@house.texas.gov,Harold.Dutton@house.texas.gov,Paul.Dyson@house.texas.gov,Lulu.Flores@house.texas.gov,Richard.Hayes@house.texas.gov,Marc.LaHood@house.texas.gov,Brooks.Landgraf@house.texas.gov,Jessica.Gonzalez@house.texas.gov,Joe.Moody@house.texas.gov,Mike.Schofield@house.texas.gov?subject=Vote%20YES%20on%20HB%203441&body=Dear%20Representative%2C%0A%0AI%20am%20writing%20to%20you%20today%20to%20ask%20you%20to%20please%20vote%20HB%203441%20favorably%20out%20of%20committee.%0A%0AThis%20bill%20would%20increase%20accountability%20for%20pharmaceutical%20companies%20and%20help%20restore%20trust%20in%20public%20health%20recommendations.%20%0A%0AThank%20you%2C%0A%5BYOUR%20NAME%5D | | | Rep. name: Sheriff Chad Bianco
subject: Why I’m Running for California Governor
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BIANCO FOR GOVERNOR 2026 FPPC # 1479095
body:
I’ve dedicated my career to protecting Californians. Now, I’m running to rebuild the California Dream
I’ve dedicated my career to protecting Californians. Now, I’m running to rebuild the California Dream.
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=195ad983c7224f0f87a1e1818dab0687&_e=6TWID7-B3uS-9lGvBXT8Sq7mETmfQPKQXlW4xYVZIGxgKFggICvIrO3o9c6SUxZpKrtg2m8XkXMlvWcJQktQi7bu25BNhkWX1JZ8EcrUHkXRgJLsklXVYySYd7GrhsaxKVJWqZkOGIjIm6EpgenpRL5ghLD8jRxEqBgIX8x8Sweji8BtPPrK32PkpKVEVHDP1E3DKZz5bbUAXcCeUX9m8wsMCn-lDmgP6VM3DeZrdbAofhykY71WM29cbe7cptaRZAdOj5LZRZtfmU51m1oPh13CM9_emX1e0WqDSP_q35U%3D)
Peter,

For the past 30 years, **I’ve dedicated my life to keeping Californians safe.** As Sheriff of Riverside County—one of the largest counties in the state—I’ve seen firsthand what happens when politicians in Sacramento put criminals ahead of law-abiding citizens.

I didn’t stand by and let it happen. I fought back. I held criminals accountable, supported law enforcement, and made Riverside County’s 2.3 million residents safer.

**But being safe is just the start.**

Once our streets are secure, families must be able to **find good jobs, start businesses, buy homes, and raise their kids in strong communities.** That’s the California Dream—and that’s what’s being destroyed by the same politicians who let crime run rampant.

But the truth is that a Sheriff can only do so much. Law enforcement officials don’t write or sign laws. Our elected representatives in Sacramento do.

That’s why I’m running for governor.

* * *
**✅ I need you with me for the bigger fight ahead. The fight to take back the Governor’s Mansion and save California:**

[**CHIP IN $35 »**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=195ad983c7224f0f87a1e1818dab0687&_e=6TWID7-B3uS-9lGvBXT8Sq7mETmfQPKQXlW4xYVZIGxgKFggICvIrO3o9c6SUxZpKrtg2m8XkXMlvWcJQktQi7bu25BNhkWX1JZ8EcrUHkXUWoHzS-3KiiqJNxFS5VRytpSX3FK6i0bXLNdaZ3dasClaP4YOsbPTD3d5eldZypInd-aiXu4wUpZ0K3XtpoypCt0uazWEXkxelqSoAE8aVSlREbPC_58dEYvr4KVv4dJYdFZyKKkjHIPHAuQHzCFLLMAEJI2NeFERB9RI9Qy-L70_rCv5_Ub2-8lic0jBNe1paPGjkask_Y22z0W0waG7)
[**CHIP IN $50 »**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=195ad983c7224f0f87a1e1818dab0687&_e=6TWID7-B3uS-9lGvBXT8Sq7mETmfQPKQXlW4xYVZIGxgKFggICvIrO3o9c6SUxZpKrtg2m8XkXMlvWcJQktQi7bu25BNhkWX1JZ8EcrUHkURw7jjUh_0wo1TG1Dve3RvEYQ3O9Jex1Vfejgl2CKWU-vRlg-EC8k5_zmRM5fHnafFdmVer2yR4gAooHRwZ2IwheJIrBZ36enM00Bk6xPyCmUrDjfb6bEZQo8vYc4dQyOkpdT9HPtGSFh1-zy744WEYFkzh9eEWBT4MK2t3fiXP4euCmPlzPvku-oiT65dZohxLhA8dtdzKCsg7jQBYkah)
[**CHIP IN ANY AMOUNT**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=195ad983c7224f0f87a1e1818dab0687&_e=6TWID7-B3uS-9lGvBXT8Sq7mETmfQPKQXlW4xYVZIGxgKFggICvIrO3o9c6SUxZpKrtg2m8XkXMlvWcJQktQi7bu25BNhkWX1JZ8EcrUHkXRgJLsklXVYySYd7GrhsaxKVJWqZkOGIjIm6EpgenpRHVCgaHfZTny5zHFKYA6IEK7mnXZT5aFkfR80WfndppHUg-AkC2cM4qZpnVrHxmLGHP0i546rzNl_p_JF2S_yt4GKUZ1a8gYGIMdgLadAiK68Egq4DkhXTD5rg8HrPid3MZJ4jsSHupJ_nxaMwa0Q4U%3D)

* * *

Sacramento needs real leadership—someone who won’t back down when the left and their special interests try to cling to power. I’ve protected Californians for 30 years.

**Now, I want to save our Golden State. I know we can take California back. Will you stand with me?**

[**FIGHT WITH ME**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=195ad983c7224f0f87a1e1818dab0687&_e=6TWID7-B3uS-9lGvBXT8Sq7mETmfQPKQXlW4xYVZIGxgKFggICvIrO3o9c6SUxZpKrtg2m8XkXMlvWcJQktQi7bu25BNhkWX1JZ8EcrUHkXRgJLsklXVYySYd7GrhsaxKVJWqZkOGIjIm6EpgenpRGXg4jbDyEVUV_wLUxmDG_EcoT6RgqfCWTbZOfAyKX8XYqJxh9o-mrQNwMVUfgkucPYfCN0WlxEG9ZWK5D8hNtrlNyq5xHg8nAZzUbaey0QmRAejsNPGepraJcWc_BUGj3c9fFt3xhXR5bFd2qiRccI%3D)
For California,

**Sheriff Chad Bianco**
Republican Candidate for California Governor

| | PAID FOR BY BIANCO FOR GOVERNOR 2026 FPPC # 1479095

## Tennessee Rep. Gino Bulso Exposes Dangerous “Assisted Reproductive Technologies” Bill
Image Credit: Adelia Kirchner / The Tennessee Conservative The Tennessee Conservative [By Olivia Lupia] – Representative Gino Bulso (R-Brentwood-District 61) is proposing a significant amendment to HB0533, which seeks to enact the “Fertility Treatment and Contraceptive Protection Act”. name: Elizabeth Nader
subject: America (and NJ) first – sorry, Europe
body:
Peter, New Jersey used to be a manufacturing powerhouse, but the industry has been in a decades-long decline. The same thing could be said for much of the country, as we have faced a gaping trade deficit (i.e. we’re
| | [ ]()
---
Peter, New Jersey used to be a manufacturing powerhouse, but the industry has been in a decades-long decline. The same thing could be said for much of the country, as we have faced a gaping trade deficit (i.e. we’re buying more than we’re selling). We were letting other countries reap the benefits of this arrangement while manufacturing jobs on our own soil declined and American workers – especially in New Jersey – took the hit. This past week, President Trump said ‘enough is enough’ and instituted a wave of reciprocal tariffs. Predictably, it sent smug and elitist European leaders into a tail spin of whining and bloviating – which is probably the clearest signal that this was the right call. Again and again, the Trump Administration has made clear that the days of us holding up the world’s safety and economies at the expense of our own are over. America first. Finally. I, for one, am optimistic that the rebirth of American manufacturing will start right here in New Jersey. As the Left takes the side of Europe (with one media figure calling the tariffs “evil”), we need a platform for common sense here in New Jersey now more than ever. I hope you’ll join us and [chip in](https://xtz223.keap-link012.com/v2/click/204a85d1ea700c2f215ed6904597a8e6/eJyNkE0LgkAQhv_LnC1LrcibRIhYHaLOsa0DLensso7SB_73tg_qUtB15nmf4Z0rMJIgzgqI4cSXIAjBA4tSGYXEM00s5GMbjKJhOPSgVHRMrW4MxNev4TdwH0fjaeQBnw06ZLNOZnm2SneLbJU70gjrbvzlGQ0mH898mWQL6LqfYqwUz1vnriFm2-C9UaFcK97a0vEHZlPHvi91VWnq1Ug19mTZ7PsW27YvtV9oEoxOJYxBKl5_yPH8FHY3CBJmYg==) so we can keep speaking loudly and boldly on behalf of YOU, the Common Sense Majority.
---
[ America First. New Jersey First. I’ll chip in right now ](https://xtz223.keap-link012.com/v2/click/faebcc74befcf3b06f6849014913f4e6/eJyNkE0LgkAQhv_LnDXTrMhbRIRYHaLOsa0DLensso7SB_731gpPBV1nnvcZ3nkAIwniNIcErnyPohF4YFEqo5B4oYmFfG2jcRyOQg8KRZeV1bWB5PE13APdOJ7MJh7wzaBD9rv5Iku3q-M63WaONMK6G395xsNp3HuWm3m6hrb9KcZS8bJx7goStjV2jXLlWvHBFo4_M5sqCQKpy1KTXyFV6MuiPg0sNs1A6iDXJBidShiDlH_-kOHtLWyfCbxmZA==)
---
Thank you in advance. For New Jersey, Elizabeth Nader

Hello, and thank you for taking the time to read this email. This message is part of an ongoing conversation between us, and I want to take a moment to ensure you have full transparency about why you received this message, how your information is handled, and what to expect from future emails. Why You’re Receiving This Email You’re receiving this email because at some point, you opted in to receive updates, news, or information on a specific topic we’ve previously discussed or shared. Whether it was through a subscription, a form submission, or another form of communication, your information was shared willingly, and we respect your decision to connect with us. If you’re wondering about the nature of our correspondence, rest assured that we aim to keep all emails relevant, timely, and free from unnecessary clutter. This includes respecting your inbox and refraining from sending irrelevant messages. Your Privacy is Important We value your trust and take your privacy very seriously. The information you provide is securely stored and never shared, sold, or used outside the purpose for which you provided it. If you ever want to review how we handle your data, feel free to contact us directly. Transparency is our priority, and we’re happy to address any questions you might have. You are always in control of the information you share. If you feel that any part of your subscription or interaction needs clarification, let us know. We’re here to provide accurate answers and ensure your satisfaction. How to Unsubscribe or Manage Your Preferences We understand that everyone’s inbox is different. If you ever find our emails no longer relevant, there’s no hard feelings. You can easily manage your email preferences or unsubscribe using the link provided below. By clicking the unsubscribe link, you’ll be taken to a page where you can either adjust your communication preferences (such as receiving fewer emails or only on specific topics) or completely remove yourself from our list. The process is straightforward, and any changes will take effect promptly. We don’t use tricks or gimmicks to keep you subscribed. Our priority is to ensure that our emails add value to your day, and if they don’t, we respect your decision to part ways. Accessibility and Communication If you have any trouble accessing the unsubscribe page, managing preferences, or understanding why you’re receiving this email, you can reach out directly to our support team at info@commonsense.org. We aim to provide a response within a reasonable time frame and address your concerns effectively. Our communication is designed to be as inclusive as possible, but we know there’s always room for improvement. If you have any feedback on how we can make our emails more accessible or relevant, please don’t hesitate to share. A Commitment to Non-Intrusive Emails We aim to create a non-intrusive communication experience. This means we won’t overwhelm your inbox with excessive messages, and we work hard to ensure our content remains clear and concise. The purpose of this email is to stay connected with you and provide updates or information that we believe is meaningful. If we ever fail to meet these standards, we encourage you to let us know. Feedback, whether positive or constructive, is always appreciated. Your thoughts help us understand how we can do better and improve our communication approach. While we cannot guarantee every suggestion will be implemented, we will take the time to carefully review and consider your input. Contact Information If you’d like to get in touch with us outside of managing your preferences, here’s how you can reach us: Email: info@commonsense.org
Phone: (866) 916-3221 (please note this is a text-only line)
Mailing Address:
Common Sense Club
165 Route 35
Red Bank, New Jersey, 07701 We strive to ensure all communication channels are open and readily available to you. Whether it’s a question, comment, or concern, our team is ready to assist. Legal Information We comply with all applicable laws and regulations regarding email communication. This includes adhering to anti-spam laws and maintaining the highest standards for consent-based communication. Your trust matters, and we work diligently to ensure every email you receive meets these requirements. If you need further clarification on our compliance policies or legal obligations, please feel free to ask. Transparency and accountability are central to our communication strategy, and we’re happy to provide additional details if needed. A Final Note Emails are one of the many ways we stay connected, but we understand they aren’t perfect for everyone. If there’s another way you’d prefer to communicate or stay updated, let us know. Whether it’s through social media, a direct call, or another channel, we’re open to finding the most effective way to share information with you. We want to emphasize that our goal is never to disrupt or clutter your inbox. Every email sent is intended to provide value, and we genuinely appreciate your time and attention. Thank you for allowing us to stay in touch with you. This footer was designed to ensure transparency, provide essential information, and give you full control over your communication preferences. We hope it meets your expectations, but if there’s anything you’d like to see improved, we’re always here to listen.
---

body:
HELP DEMS WIN TWO HOUSE SEATS IN FLORIDA >>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111724_1]
Peter, we’ve just received breaking news out of Florida:
Governor Ron DeSantis has officially called on the Secretary of State to schedule TWO special elections on a RAPIDLY accelerated timeline.
To put it simply: Democrats now have a limited time to prepare to win TWO MORE HOUSE SEATS being vacated by Trump cabinet appointments
These special elections will determine the future of FL-01 , vacated by Matt Gaetz (reportedly stepping up as Trump’s pick for Attorney General), and FL-06 , soon to be left by Mike Waltz (rumored to become Trump’s National Security Advisor).
Flipping these suits blue IS possible - but only if we start preparing NOW. Will you help us build up a warchest of funding to rush to the Democrats who run for these seats? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111724_1]

Make no mistake: DeSantis and his allies are trying to fast-track these elections to catch Democrats off guard.
They know they need to secure these seats quickly to maintain their MAGA control of Congress.
We CANNOT let them get away with it!
Chip in NOW so we can have a stash of funds ready to go when Democrats put up a candidate for these seats >>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111724_1]
Thanks for taking action,

name: Senate Republicans
subject: We want to hear from you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
15 minutes to respond >>

Friend,

We have a _**[critical](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zgs0202bqpk06rdq591cdklchvisthsnpsqbhsg0_rp22sh2s8i6eob1chj64pb2clh2bs)**_ question for you.

With President Trump back in office and the Senate now under Republican control, our party is taking bold action to secure the border, deport illegal immigrants, and cut wasteful government spending.

For the first time in a long time, the American people are being put first again--and there's no turning back.

Now, it's time to hear from you:

**[IF THE ELECTION WERE HELD TODAY, WOULD YOU VOTE REPUBLICAN OR DEMOCRAT?](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zbahlh6lmrj2daa85mim59ok1bv2kuf77j8khstg_rp22sh2s8i6eob1chj64pb2clh2bs)**

******************************************************[REPUBLICAN](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z8s76v6ur7b4i1ilf1s6rsqfn1ro7hp2n01g5kk8_rp22sh2s8i6eob1chj64pb2clh2bs)******************************************************

******************************************************[DEMOCRAT](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zdqg0b4v05hv7v8t91ev53bpi6t6lqkonmvuukc0_rp22sh2s8i6eob1chj64pb2clh2bs)******************************************************

******************************************************[TAKE THE POLL>>](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zmoal1gjbc09c64u91kdk74bq6mkd2lk4atlndb0_rp22sh2s8i6eob1chj64pb2clh2bs)******************************************************

EVERY vote will be crucial in the midterms and beyond, that's why we need to know if we can count on you in the fight against the radical Left

Friend, THOUSANDS of patriots have already taken the poll, but we still need **[YOUR RESPONSE](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z17is74t39f9cesu2htmts6r56vvt988cak4uae8_rp22sh2s8i6eob1chj64pb2clh2bs)**. Do not let us down.

******************************************************[TAKE THE POLL>>](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zknvjd3t5el7foav5pm6bi4v4co2hnoq4bsvt120_rp22sh2s8i6eob1chj64pb2clh2bs)******************************************************

name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: A rundown of my first Michigan work week
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
body:
# [](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/188?t=1&akid=2265%2E135344%2EXh6Q1t)

After 10 weeks as a senator, I went on my first state work period back home in Michigan. I was able to squeeze in 23 events in 7 days all across the state. Here’s the quick rundown for you:
[](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-apr25.NJ6rxh&refcode=em250408o&t=10&akid=2265%2E135344%2EXh6Q1t)
I spoke with business leaders about the economic relationship between Michigan and Canada. Tariffs are an important tool in the toolbox, but we cannot treat Canada like China. Michigan is poised to be the most impacted state in the country, from our manufactures to our farmers, and they will very simply result in higher costs for consumers, especially for cars and groceries.
[](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-apr25.NJ6rxh&refcode=em250408o&t=11&akid=2265%2E135344%2EXh6Q1t)
I also met with local leaders and business owners in Macomb County — one of our great manufacturing centers, especially on defense. There is significant uncertainty for our companies and their employees. Our conversations varied from how I can help them navigate the changes in Congress to how federal cuts could raise costs for local businesses to support a fighter mission at Selfridge Air Force Base.
[](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-apr25.NJ6rxh&refcode=em250408o&t=12&akid=2265%2E135344%2EXh6Q1t)
In West Michigan, I held a farmers town hall, and heard from locals about high input costs, including labor, and their concerns about how trade wars could cause our farmers to lose market share and possibly increase the price of our food.
I ended the week in Oakland County with a productive town hall meeting with Michigan veterans to hear their concerns about the cuts to the VA from DOGE and Elon Musk. The conversation fit squarely into the broader theme of the week: average Michiganders from all walks of life feeling nervous about the status of Michigan’s economy AND the status of their own pocketbooks.
It’s clear to me that everyone in Michigan is paying close attention to this administration’s reckless approach to change and how it will impact their lives directly.
Because we know when D.C. sneezes, Michigan catches a cold – that’s why it’s never been more important for folks like you to speak up, tell your stories, and talk about how these cuts impact people like you.
I hope you continue to engage with me and elected leaders in Washington, Michigan, and wherever you live to share your concerns and ask for action. Your stories and input are critical to relay in DC to ensure that some of the recklessness we’ve seen is pushed back on.
Thanks for reading,
Elissa Slotkin
* * *

👋🏻 **Wait, before you click away!** 🇺🇸
United States Senators often keep on fundraising after their election ends, but Elissa doesn't want to keep bombarding your inbox with fundraising requests.
What we will do is send you good, timely content - like this message. Stuff you might open without dread.
But she does hope you may consider making a small contribution so we don't have to send those mass fundraising emails while Elissa is focused on her work as a Senator.
[**Make a contribution to Elissa Slotkin's campaign here.**](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-apr25.NJ6rxh&refcode=em250408o&t=13&akid=2265%2E135344%2EXh6Q1t)

In the week since Trump announced his sweeping tariff policy, the White House has been unclear if the goal of the tariffs is to pressure other countries into lowering trade barriers or to raise them even higher in the pursuit of reshoring American manufacturing.
Whatever the case may be, the markets are bleeding with one of the worst declines since the start of the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.
Since the announcement:
* $6 trillion in U.S. household wealth wiped out
* Dow Jones down 9.2%
* S&P 500 down 10.5% [(the worst three-day stretch since 1987)](https://substack.com/redirect/1ce5f409-f02a-4562-9444-28d675d3ac45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* Nasdaq down 11.4%
* China imposed a 34% retaliatory tariff on U.S. goods

The majority of Americans have their retirement funds tied to the stock market. Tariffs like these function as a tax on American consumers. Not only will prices go up, but the stock market drops will drive down growth, shrinking retirement accounts and investment portfolios. The rising costs of regular goods and necessities will hurt the low- and middle-income classes far more as they will be forced to dip into debt, or their savings, to make ends meet. Personal credit card debt is at its highest ever, with delinquencies at a 15-year high. As businesses look to cut costs, we may also see rising unemployment and layoffs.
These are not just market disruptions. These are the kinds of policy shifts that undermine public confidence in the system itself. And that, in the long run, may be the most damaging consequence of all.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6a114d98-4c7d-4d72-bb3c-30744069c190?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wYXRyaWNrbXVycGh5cGEuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FjY291bnQiLCJwIjoxNjA4NjEzMjAsInMiOjQzOTkxMzMsImYiOnRydWUsInUiOjkwNTAxMTkyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDQxMjAxMDMsImV4cCI6MTc0NjcxMjEwMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTAiLCJzdWIiOiJsaW5rLXJlZGlyZWN0In0.mymhxzZNf5WSLp2g2MQ57lPsNfhSI6bczH5uWn-sAzw?)
**A Time for Unity and Problem Solving**
Beneath the noise of economic headlines lies a more persistent issue: our deepening political division. We've reached a point where Americans increasingly view one another not as neighbors but as enemies divided by partisanship and distrust.
America is at her best when we're united; even our currency's motto is 'One nation under God.' But foreign entities have spent millions for years trying to divide us from within, propping up the most divisive voices on the far right and far left. Hyperpartisanship has hollowed out trust in our government, corporations, and churches, straining even the most basic bonds of confidence and faith in our systems and each other.
According to recent polling, there's now an 87% partisan gap in presidential approval ratings, up two points from President Biden's term in office. The highest in modern history. It reflects a deeper erosion of how far we've drifted from our shared civic reality. We should voice our concerns when we disagree with our elected officials and their policies, but we also need to remember: We're still in this together. If we can't see past party lines to support each other as Americans, we risk losing more than just wealth--_we risk losing the democratic spirit that holds us together._
In moments like this, unity isn't a platitude, it's a basic principle for survival. That starts with leadership, but it also starts with us. It starts not with grand gestures but with small ones: listening before reacting, questioning before condemning, and remembering that behind every ballot is a person putting themselves out there to do what they think is right.
This past weekend, I was reminded of this as my son and I were helping clear a house next to a man with a 'Trump 2024' hat on in our small 9,000-person Bristol Borough in the swing county of Bucks. Our neighbors had just lost their father and two sons days earlier on the Amtrak train tracks, as the father and brother tried to help save their youngest son from taking his own life. It was tragic, and the family asked for our help moving stuff.
Politics didn't come up, we were just standing shoulder-to-shoulder in a human chain, helping the distraught family carry stuff out. It was important for my son to see, but also for me. When you get down to the division happening in America, just look to those trying to unify us, not divide us.
We don't have to agree on everything. But we do have to believe that it's always best to have empathy and humility, and while history doesn't repeat itself, it often rhymes. It will get better.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f69649fa-10a9-4147-8874-565445aa1c39?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Financial Preparation for Military Families**
With market volatility on the rise, it's more important than ever to be prepared for financial uncertainty, especially for veteran families, who face unique challenges.
This past Friday, I sat down with financial advisor Sara Gardner on my _Yahoo Finance_ podcast, _Warrior Money._ We talked about how to help military families build long-term financial stability, even in uncertain times, and how important it is to have a plan.
Most people probably don't know that 24% of military spouses are unemployed--a staggering figure that highlights the financial challenges many veteran families face. Sara's approach is all about meeting families where they are. Whether it's balancing a checkbook or exploring investment strategies, there's no one-size-fits-all approach to personal finance.
Some key takeaways we discussed:
* Build savings and avoid high-interest credit
* Tap into support services specific to each military branch
* Manage and pay off debt
* Understand core financial instruments like IRAs and employer benefits (like 401(k)s)
* Set clear financial goals based on your unique income and expenses

Most importantly, we discussed the value of volunteering, not just as a way to give back, but as a path to community, mentorship, and long-term support.
The need to come together and form real-world bonds has never been more important.
-- Patrick
_The Honorable Patrick J. Murphy is a Wharton lecturer, Vetrepreneur, and the 32nd Army Under Secretary after earning the Bronze Star for service in Baghdad, Iraq as an All-American with the 82nd Airborne Division --@PatrickMurphyPA on Instagram and Twitter._

## Tariffs Are a Reason to Cut Taxes and Red Tape
If there’s one thing supporters and opponents of President Donald Trump’s tariffs should agree on, it’s the need to unchain prosperity at home while international trade is in flux. That means renewing the president’s first-term tax cuts, for a start, but it also calls for sweeping regulatory reform—a DOGE-like approach to slice the red tape [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/vQW4lw8LvpSBPaSnsTkSqfECmqukCknkZn-WBGXTNn8wPmVVZeWKir_yYRmC5rAF1GKFw-vu2TNc8STtmzevPCWOH4P84khp8KBDuhOV_u0iFB3a9bsKr59ZydaBhpvtytcW0NKqsrkUrMvTS0xTcbRUdGC0bXuOqapM2iTDP8tNQLEezFqdrjgKdyajoQUy7OlDRSKWm7CxAKIR7gk4IxhEOFxqCWTEi1cbYpRW4ZOuNJA4rGH2XKt0lSW7MY40TwtrRDpwZZuVXwX_dXgCBDovqKXjUjYRY1L8Isz6BEPtXQK7CFSeGa9ISnU-Is9aYd8UQMj2Rw/pWHjT2lYr3qsM7ekEU2t3aroNKEwQ-XP/7)

## ‘Raiders of the Lost Ark’-Type Footage Exposes Mine Where Fed Retirements Are Processed – RedState
The bombshell reports came quickly after Donald Trump won the presidential election, took back the Oval Office, and directed Elon Musk to head the Department of Government Efficiency. Musk, the CEO of both SpaceX and Tesla, quickly uncovered, along with his DOGE team, staggering amounts of waste, fraud and abuse in the federal government, [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/qMTm9kcmLp1Jq3ROO_XroYnaHbZEeJO0QEUZ5tBUGy3NEfzPdMm5d8OqtzPvFDp64tHqXVdfOOdm6UsPkkHv4Y61IgRlg2jjo-_TTjtsoRR4p_Jh78RA6f7kG4hcbfRBKVLlo57A0kaQcdUErz6iKMi6OajmDg_iGw6c8VfIdOzgCtrzb7EI_h-Cl1hAuI2p6nrRBKRiMRkjtsDt5_cmVZkQc5QEZnT7whElqscNKZg-7eHVYd8hCf38prjGKDG0K3ShGIn8Y6SG64wGNUzyIN1x7Ew276vZuqu3o4R0dAT53c_tMTg0J-uLMhMKtQ3RnQF9FD0e2sh599E_4o8Ieqjkk8DjY1pG8iLSHKMKkPPRy3fhk-J6i49SfVQjIUtVPgFLguv99g/4xG5Ci0MvLDfKWdjsWvGCavq69_aGSNe/7)

April 7, 2025: 11:00 p.m.: April 7, 2025: DOT, DOI, DOE, FEMA Highlights The Ohio Department of Transportation announced 172 new road projects in Northeast Ohio, totaling nearly $900 million, including major bridge repaving at the I-480/I-77 interchange. name: Amanda Chase
subject: After Action Report
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase 2025
body:
Thank you
| | |
---
| |
---

---
| |
---

---
|

---
| THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF VIRGINIA HAS ADVISED THAT WE CAME UP SHORT
---
|
# UPDATE
---
|
# THANK YOU
---
|
Dear Hello My apologies for the delay in my report to you. The Republican Party of Virginia legally had until today to announce its nominees, but finished their counts early. They made the announcement early Saturday morning, that Winsome Sears is our Republican nominee for Governor. We were out of state at a wedding this weekend and then I unexpectedly became ill.
I'm feeling better now and wanted to touch base with many of you who have asked what happened, after hearing the Republican Party of Virginia announce on Saturday that Lieutenant Governor Winsome Sears is our Republican nominee.
We received a phone call early Saturday morning that we fell short on the 10,000 verified signatures, 400 in each Congressional District legal requirement to make it on the ballot for the Republican Primary to be held this coming June 17, 2025. We thought we had submitted close to the required 10,000 signatures, with the incredible number of Fed Ex envelopes that came in within hours of the deadline, but that we did not have time to verify the signatures.
I knew we would be close, so I submitted our petitions along with the required $3,500 filing fee. That's a lot of money to me. Had I not thought we were close, I would not have submitted the petitions.
Once we submitted our petitions to the Virginia State Board of Elections on Thursday at approximately 4:40pm, we were advised that our sealed box would be picked up by the Republican Party of Virginia, who would be given the sole responsibility for counting our signatures. Every signature and petition that is filed is carefully scrutinized to make sure the voter is an eligible voter. Unfortunately, because so many of the petitions came in at the ninth hour, our volunteer campaign staff was unable to verify many of the signatures that came in via Fed Ex as we were walking out the door to drive to Richmond. We simply ran out of time.
Although we ultimately fell short, our efforts were impressive for a grassroots organization, and as a result I plan to consider another run for governor in 4 years. There was much excitement about our campaign in such a short period of time.
But next time, we will start January 1, which is the first day a campaign can begin collecting signatures. We will take the next 3 years traveling the Commonwealth and getting to know the needs of each region.
Thanks to all of our committed campaign volunteers who worked tirelessly over the past 5 weeks. We were trying to accomplish what most campaigns would accomplish over 3 full months into 3 full weeks.
We knew our mission would be difficult as we were starting our campaign at the ninth hour. But knowing the stakes for winning Virginia, we launched our campaign full speed ahead.
At this time, I'm encouraging my supporters to vote for and support Lieutenant Governor Winsome Sears for Governor against the Democrat candidate Abigail Spanberger. I'm encouraging the LG to reach out to the thousands of Trump supporters across our Commonwealth. Many of us want to see our next Virginia Governor do what President Trump is doing at the national level.
That said, we can't afford another repeat of 2020 with a Democrat Trifecta. So please don't stay home. Get out and vote for our Republican nominee. Next time, we will be much better prepared.

---
| |
---
|
---
| |
---
| |
---
|
---
|
# PLEASE GIVE GENEROUSLY
---
| It costs money to campaign across Virginia. Gas money, hotel expenses, event fees, sending emails and maintaining websites cost money. I will continue to publish my Honey Badger Report and travel our beautiful Commonwealth speaking to different groups about true election integrity, true school choice for all students and making Virginia prosperous again. To support my efforts, please donate below.
---
| | [ DONATE HERE ](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Kt9cxNObayLvSy09--fPHQ0Xhz88xhwjCVsZFj1iWyIMEcerFtWLOuIjWTs10pSI9_NyamSuSoAaRBnQj-s3Q9B0Kwzc4L1lSSJ8f53Maw49bKRhq-4Fuv0h11YCo3y5mvVXoZ5rvfabl6IS3wpLYeDpTx1ltMiW2RPsqxrY96scdhJUy5Ua4X5PYXfc_b1MdIj8JULfNF9hdkJr-qTlSnuAEZFL_oB4&c=ywnET9nM8nTLHRir78dlA1W29OsPBgbgQ9Vz_nHU6lilgrubEbWncQ==&ch=rNieCzCE8d5vURd4i2dMdOrEPLURpJCQVK9ep9Ht_JB1vudt-UoCYA==)
---
| Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase 2025

| | | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Box 250 | Appomattox, VA 24522 US

clean_body: Only the weak will fail. This is an economic revolution, and we will win! Hang tough. It won’t be easy, but the end result will be historic! The United States has a chance to do something that should have been done decades ago. | | | | | | DON’T BE A PANICAN! Friend, (Definition from the Trump Dictionary) PANICAN: A new party based on Weak and Stupid people! We have massive Financial Deficits with China, the European Union, and many others. The only way this problem can be cured is with the HISTORIC TRUMP TARIFFS , which are now bringing Tens of Billions of Dollars into the U.S.A. They are already in effect, and a beautiful thing to behold. The Surplus with these Countries has grown during the “Presidency” of Sleepy Joe Biden. We are going to reverse it, and reverse it QUICKLY. Some day people will realize that Tariffs, for the United States of America, are a very beautiful thing! THIS IS AN ECONOMIC REVOLUTION, AND WE WILL WIN. HANG TOUGH, it won’t be easy, but the end result will be historic. We will, MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!!! There are many PANICANS out there, but you’ve never let me down. BEFORE I make my next move for America, I need to know if you support me... Sincerely, Donald J. Erica is a well-respected scientist, businesswoman, and advocate for marginalized communities. She believes in building a community that’s safe, sustainable, and strong, where everyone has a voice, and every resident, regardless of age, race, gender, or background, feels welcome and protected.

Her Democratic opponent, Barry Cassidy, on the other hand, is running as a Democrat, but his words and actions tell a shocking story. **He is a proud white nationalist who routinely uses racist and anti-LGBTQ language. He openly aligns with MAGA extremism.**
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105419977/538283768/-459442197?refcode=EM_F_250407Deuso_N_CD_NO_10_G_N_P1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjE2MGU4N2MyLTgxMTQtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=MPxXLs1m6MuEEYglq4lEf78ggUFzjs4QpVhtQHofp14=)
---
That’s not all: If Erica wins the primary, she will be facing off against Republican Richard Bryant, **a far-right extremist and unapologetic Trump supporter.** He uses his online platform to spew misogyny, anti-trans rhetoric, and racist commentary. Additionally, he is known to mock women for their appearance, attack the LGBTQ+ community, and spread harmful lies about climate change and immigrants. He advocates for violence, promotes mass deportation, and supports banning books and suppressing votes. As Erica’s campaign has stated, **“This isn’t political disagreement—this is dangerous, dehumanizing extremism.”**

_In Erica’s own words: “The constituency in Downingtown is diverse, educated, and proud of our borough’s progress and growing inclusivity. Voters here care about good schools, safe streets, responsible development, and a local government that reflects the people it serves. They want a mayor who listens, who leads with compassion, and who is focused on building a better future.

I bring my whole self to this work—including my perspective as a trans woman who’s had to fight to be taken seriously in rooms where decisions get made. That makes me more determined to lead with purpose and to make sure every woman in Downingtown knows her voice matters, and her community has her back. Because when we take care of women, we’re taking care of the whole town.”_

**But Erica needs our help.**

[**Her campaign needs to raise $17,000 to fully fund all of their programs. Through our incredible community of grassroots supporters, we know we can help her get there. Rush support to Erica’s campaign by pitching in a split $20.25 or more contribution immediately →**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105419989/538283789/-459442197?refcode=EM_F_250407Deuso_N_CD_NO_10_G_N_L2&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjE2MGU4N2MyLTgxMTQtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=MPxXLs1m6MuEEYglq4lEf78ggUFzjs4QpVhtQHofp14=)

Thank you,

Colleen Davis
Endorsements Manager
Her Bold Move

Additionally, he is known to mock women for their appearance, attack the LGBTQ+ community, and spread harmful lies about climate change and immigrants. He advocates for violence, promotes mass deportation, and supports banning books and suppressing votes. As Erica’s campaign has stated, “This isn’t political disagreement—this is dangerous, dehumanizing extremism.” In Erica’s own words: “The constituency in Downingtown is diverse, educated, and proud of our borough’s progress and growing inclusivity. Voters here care about good schools, safe streets, responsible development, and a local government that reflects the people it serves. They want a mayor who listens, who leads with compassion, and who is focused on building a better future. I bring my whole self to this work—including my perspective as a trans woman who’s had to fight to be taken seriously in rooms where decisions get made. That makes me more determined to lead with purpose and to make sure every woman in Downingtown knows her voice matters, and her community has her back. Because when we take care of women, we’re taking care of the whole town.” But Erica needs our help. = Thank you, Colleen Davis Endorsements Manager Her Bold Move | | Her Bold Move is the fastest-growing support network for women running for political office in America. As dangerous laws about women get passed in states with the lowest numbers of women in government (11%, 12%....), we're BREAKING the male supermajorities across those states. Because men shouldn't be making laws about women's bodies. Support this important work with a contribution today! = | | | | | Paid for by Her Bold Move Action. Her Bold Move One Park Row 5th Floor Providence, RI 02903 United States This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you believe this email was sent in error, please contact us at info@herboldmove.org. We'd hate to see you go, but if you must, you can . To learn more about Her Bold Move Action, please visit us at herboldmove.org.

name: Reid Rasner, CEO
subject: How much time can your business spend doing this?
body:
| Hi Peter, How long does it typically take to find the right talent for your business? The answer can vary depending on the role, but on average, it takes 36 days to fill non-leadership positions — and even longer for executives.1 And once hired, it can take up to six months for leaders to fully settle into their roles.1 These timelines can feel daunting, but with proactive business succession planning, you can reduce delays, secure the best-fit talent, and ensure smooth transitions when leadership changes arise. **[Click here to explore tips for succession planning!](https://financeinsights.net/bg6pFNN04FljOekSwb8AaTZrxxP?email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)**
---
|
---
Whether you're managing a startup, a small business, or a larger company, a solid succession plan can serve as a safety net to prevent extended vacancies and keep your business moving forward. Succession planning isn't just about avoiding disruptions—it's also an opportunity to strengthen your business, foster leadership pipelines, and position your company for long-term success. **[Discover how succession planning can support your business's future by clicking here!](https://financeinsights.net/bg6pFNN04FljOekSwb8AaTZrxxP?email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)** Here's to proactive planning and smooth transitions,
---
**Reid Rasner - CEO**
Omnivest Financial
(307) 337-1223
[https://www.omnivestfinancial.com](https://snpy.io?djJ7InVybCI6Imh0dHBzOlwvXC93d3cub21uaXZlc3RmaW5hbmNpYWwuY29tIiwiaGFzaCI6Im5ONjdmVlZFMnVyZUtaZCIsImNvbnRhY3RfZW1haWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9)
* * *
---
P.S. What type of crossroads moments have you faced running your business? What decisions did you have to make and what happened after? Hit "reply" and share your story. I'd love to hear about your experience making tough choices, how it turned out, and what you learned in the process.
---
Sources

1\. InStride, 2024 [URL: [https://www.instride.com/insights/succession-planning/](https://snpy.io?djJ7InVybCI6Imh0dHBzOlwvXC93d3cuaW5zdHJpZGUuY29tXC9pbnNpZ2h0c1wvc3VjY2Vzc2lvbi1wbGFubmluZ1wvIiwiaGFzaCI6Im5ONjdmVlZFMnVyZUtaZCIsImNvbnRhY3RfZW1haWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9)]

What decisions did you have to make and what happened after? Hit "reply" and share your story. I'd love to hear about your experience making tough choices, how it turned out, and what you learned in the process. Sources 1\. _As I’m sure you’ve seen on the news,_judges_ have now gone rogue and are REVOLTING. They’re trying to handcuff President Trump to stop him from demolishing the Deep State._

And they’d love nothing more than to see his former lawyer – yours truly – behind bars.

**In fact, my own fate now rests in the hands of a judge who will decide whether I live in a prison cell until I am 100 years old.**

Which is why, at 80 years old, I’m __still__ FIGHTING BACK.

When these leftist crooks first had me booked, they filed my mugshot under **booking # 2313757** ...
---
And today, to show them that good and honest Americans like _YOU_ are also fighters who refuse to stand by as they destroy the life of an INNOCENT man all for defending our 45th and 47th President, I’m humbly asking for your support to raise **$231,375.70** for my Legal Defense Trust to WIN the trial of my life and the fight for my freedom and justice.

subject: Case closed?

| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)

---

Two things are true:
1. DOGE is ineffective, harming Americans, damaging faith in our government at home and our standing abroad, and [run by a eugenicist](https://substack.com/redirect/e397a3d9-10ed-4162-a322-10425c4e6e36?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and generally bad person.
2. Government is bloated, and it's not particularly efficient.

Want an example? Look no further than the counties in Republican-controlled Missouri.1
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTYwMDk5NDUyLCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzQ0MTIxNTU4LCJleHAiOjE3NDY3MTM1NTgsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.-5yxMeG4WCeEoUO0uEsCpxGuhQlbg5cOh5MBRQO1o0g?)
* * *
> _**Article summary:**_
> * _Missouri 's centuries-old system of county government makes it harder to deliver high-quality services to its residents. Lots of states face similar issues._
> * _A lack of accountability --no recent public data, few journalists, exemptions from reporting requirements--makes it hard to know why certain counties are so much less efficient than others._
> * _If Missouri 's Republican supermajority were serious about efficiency, this is the first place they'd look. (But of course, they aren't serious.)_
>

* * *
## We have way too many counties
Missouri has 115 counties, [fifth-most of any state in the country](https://substack.com/redirect/13fa1ed9-adb5-40d1-98f1-5bd43dfcb04a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). That stat alone indicates serious bloat, but when you dig into the data, you start to realize how absurd it is that Missouri has that many counties:
* 56 counties--nearly half!--have [smaller populations than they did in 1900](https://substack.com/redirect/14bff334-2516-4400-8e83-ec8b1e7e3df3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).2 (For context, the U.S. population has [grown 4.5× in that time](https://substack.com/redirect/3d85427a-7e53-4ee1-bc8e-6ef36802a5ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)
* 60 counties have populations smaller than 20,000. Those make up the vast majority of Missouri's counties that have been shrinking for the last century-plus.

Once upon a time--when James Buchanan was President, and the [Transcontinental Railroad](https://substack.com/redirect/57b92638-1530-4c3a-97eb-2610f50b5aba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was still a decade or two away--this made sense. In the horse-and-buggy era, even if you had paved roads, you [weren't moving faster than 5-8 mph](https://substack.com/redirect/c2daa439-f505-4882-b84e-74f7adcc6d4a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).3 And since [hardly any of the country's roads were paved](https://substack.com/redirect/b7a9a018-dd4a-4850-9d2a-60de1801c592?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) back then, in practice you were moving even more slowly than that.
In that era, the [services that a county provided](https://substack.com/redirect/29db506a-9cbf-448e-9576-133a586cfcb0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--everything from surveying to getting licenses to recording transactions to registering births and deaths--you _had_ to be close to your county seat. Otherwise, there was no other way to do any of this without spending days on the road.
## The need for this many counties is entirely obsolete
The problem now is that this whole set-up is totally vestigial:
* You can reach your county offices by phone, and [98% of Americans have a phone](https://substack.com/redirect/5df10228-39e9-40ab-bd28-803a1a437f40?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* You can access many records online. [96% of Americans use the internet](https://substack.com/redirect/7cd7674d-f63c-47dc-bb24-dada9838b6b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [90% of Americans have broadband internet access](https://substack.com/redirect/be0bca42-2d4c-4703-8ca9-0fc7067738dd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).4
* You can reach your county officials by email, and well over [90% of Americans have email addresses](https://substack.com/redirect/1a64b43f-4b19-4148-be74-e7bb68b50ea1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).5
* If all else fails, and you have to go to your county seat in person, you can get there in an automobile, and [94% of Missourians have a personal vehicle](https://substack.com/redirect/e1d74e68-5724-48bd-b86d-386a4f6decd4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). (In rural Missouri, the rates are even higher than that.) You can drive there on roads where the [speed limits are at least 50 mph](https://substack.com/redirect/82aba5ea-b265-44ce-9679-0c25887934bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and usually much higher.

We've set up an entire system of government that works really well for people living in 1861, back when Missouri's [newest](https://substack.com/redirect/0fb6cc49-4aad-4d23-899b-33754f874feb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [least populous county](https://substack.com/redirect/9e5a7b16-b7dd-47a8-9edd-278dee8e668d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was formed.
But for people living in 2025? We're still using the exact same structure of government.6 It's costly and it's not necessary.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=160099452&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYwMDk5NDUyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDQxMjE1NTgsImV4cCI6MTc0NjcxMzU1OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.A6BSmqIcW6d7AN51TN-zZIDW-ABV3eFgM5qm42hteeA)
## Having this many counties is inefficient, wasteful, and dangerous
Here's a comparison between some of the state's smallest counties and its largest.7
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/dbba8048-0eaa-43c8-a35b-c51fea4989e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
People in rural Missouri [make meaningfully less than people in urban Missouri](https://substack.com/redirect/85ee06f4-b0fe-41fb-9123-c00653a512fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And across rural Missouri, counties are spending significantly more (as a percentage of county per capita income) than cities.
**The system we have now is inefficient, wasteful, and dangerous.** This can and should be fixed.
#### **Here 's why it's inefficient**.
There are certain fixed costs associated with running counties: maintaining a county courthouse and paying 13 elected officials,8 for instance. If five small counties merged, they could cut these costs by 80%. But the inefficiency is much deeper than that.
Take, for instance, the roads.
* [21% of rural roads in Missouri are in poor condition](https://substack.com/redirect/9fc6b4e9-1b16-4cda-b78d-f38ecc6b7e84?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the seventh-highest rate in the country.9
* Consequently, Missouri has some of the country's [highest rates of rural non-interstate traffic fatalities](https://substack.com/redirect/af733935-1ea1-489d-9c9c-54f5c551f2fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Even the smallest counties have [many 100s of road miles](https://substack.com/redirect/7ef481d9-86a3-45df-b868-9f2db15ac0b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that they maintain.10

To get roads repaired, counties need to contract out that work. But with so many small counties:
1. Counties are forced to bid against other small counties, meaning that critical work doesn't get done for longer.
2. Counties lose the critical negotiating leverage with more volume, and they probably end up paying more.

**Because of the poor state of Missouri 's rural county roads, people are needlessly dying**. With larger counties, we could address those issues faster and more cost-effectively.
#### **Here 's why it's wasteful**.
Let's compare Missouri's elected coroners.11
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/722e3e97-d2c5-4438-b4df-acf27d9bc624?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
First of all, I have no idea why Clark County pays its coroner so much more, given that it has roughly the same population as Putnam and Sullivan Counties.
Second, I also wonder why Sullivan and Clark Counties, whose collective population is half of McDonald County's, _each_ pays its coroner more than McDonald County.
Finally, I question whether each of these counties can even find qualified coroners.
Missouri's statutory qualifications for the position [are shockingly lax](https://substack.com/redirect/78c8b14b-70f3-474a-b1a8-60faa634a987?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), requiring no knowledge of what it takes to be a coroner. There just aren't that many people I'd consider qualified for the role, since there are only [61,000 funeral service workers](https://substack.com/redirect/74937eb3-e4d7-4c51-b7fd-3673db131113?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the whole country.
I'm sure that the coroners of Putnam, Sullivan, Clark, and McDonald Counties are fine people.12 But statistically speaking, when 1-in-5,600 Americans is qualified to be a coroner, and when a [tiny fraction of Americans want to run for public office](https://substack.com/redirect/56502a5c-73e5-4f88-85ff-9755d9a3b07c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg),13 the odds that they live in one of those counties _and_ want to run for office are pretty darn low.
With larger counties, the pool for these technical roles is larger. [Training and support for coroners is a real issue](https://substack.com/redirect/e164f90e-2cb1-4ff5-98cb-dbeef3f34257?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); the Missouri Legislature, to its credit, recognizes this. Merging the state's smaller counties will lead to a larger pool of more qualified people running for office.
We can joke about this with coroners. But when it comes to Prosecuting Attorneys or Treasurers--both elected positions--this is a very real and very acute problem, where (living) people will suffer if we don't have qualified people in those roles.
#### **Here 's why it's dangerous**.
I already talked about the fact that Missouri's inadequate roads are literally killing people. This goes beyond that.
* I wanted to run an analysis on whether Missouri's rural counties were systematically spending more. But I couldn't do that. Why? Only a small percentage of Missouri's counties have [up-to-date data with the auditor's office](https://substack.com/redirect/04651d47-a664-4270-be85-4e57207e10b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), because for no obvious reason, **[counties are literally exempt](https://substack.com/redirect/b3ad1dd7-37a0-46ab-9c40-846af0d04ff7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from certain reporting requirements with the State Auditor**.14
* Large swaths of Missouri, like the rest of the country, are [increasingly news deserts](https://substack.com/redirect/f7edee94-49d3-4c8f-a03b-7af827b9ef4a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) without robust local reporting. In all of Missouri, there [are only 580 reporters](https://substack.com/redirect/4a2229cc-2325-4871-85ef-7f80ed5e4c41?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), one of the lowest ratios of reporters-to-residents in the country. In the northern part of the state,15 there are just 30.16

Why does this matter? Because without good data and without proper reporting, **it 's extremely hard to hold all of these local governments accountable**--either to responsible spending or to fend off corruption or both.
Who's reviewing contracts to make sure there isn't self-dealing? Who's there to explain why Chariton County is _so_ much less efficient than Linn County, its direct neighbor to the north?
With this much inefficiency, no one is being properly held to account. And the way that Missouri has structured its governments is leading to less efficient spending, less accountability, and [more corruption](https://substack.com/redirect/7d4aaa82-7317-4189-9ea7-6d78b2a461ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=160099452&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYwMDk5NDUyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDQxMjE1NTgsImV4cCI6MTc0NjcxMzU1OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.A6BSmqIcW6d7AN51TN-zZIDW-ABV3eFgM5qm42hteeA)
## This issue isn't limited to Missouri
I'm using Missouri as an example, but this is true just about everywhere in the country.
Hardly anyone has done anything to reconcile inefficient and outdated county governments [in more than a century](https://substack.com/redirect/def8d754-14c6-4da7-8541-4635cdb1865e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).17 I'm more familiar with Missouri than other states, but it's clear to me that other states are flailing around with the same bloated governments too.
## With the right reforms, government _can_ work better
There is a remarkable lack of imagination in government today. Our bloated, antiquated, dispersed systems of government are inefficient, dangerous, and bad for residents.
In the grand scheme of things, does it matter whether a small county pays its coroner $16,000 or $52,000? No, not really.
What does matter: **our systems are not well set up to deliver the best services possible to people** , around the state and around the country.
This gets at the farce of DOGE: they [aren't doing anything to meaningfully cut federal spending](https://substack.com/redirect/e3083d79-31dc-42d6-816e-6561368493cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And unless they improve laws and processes, which they're not, what they're doing _will_ make federal programs worse, and people will suffer as a result.
Urban areas have their problems too, [something I've written about before](https://substack.com/redirect/f4b1fa44-2dbc-4d31-8039-9e9b3ac6b3d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). **Democrats are falling short in making government work well** , and that's hurting Democratic electoral hopes everywhere. People are right to be angry about what they're seeing in some of the country's bluest states.
No matter where you are, **it 's essential that we do more to make government efficient**. Democrats _must_ be talking more about that--especially if the Trump Administration is [needlessly barreling us towards a recession](https://substack.com/redirect/83d2f3de-69e7-4d90-8031-c50327203b96?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
But since Republicans control Missouri, and since [Missouri's](https://substack.com/redirect/dbb9f71c-3443-4521-ae8e-565803a3c074?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [elected](https://substack.com/redirect/f2835766-694c-4435-8a65-a0edb19a694e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [Republicans](https://substack.com/redirect/9920b5a9-ad41-4e11-a2f6-8d4d6ac1e093?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [are](https://substack.com/redirect/33d8f83a-240e-45d0-83a5-618ee5062a64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [so](https://substack.com/redirect/a3dd9779-c093-4e7c-a436-3556b7538243?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [keen](https://substack.com/redirect/6dfade85-a1b4-4f28-bc29-8f89128cff6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [on](https://substack.com/redirect/ef269d28-da7c-40c1-a008-5b363e6ba4c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [attacking](https://substack.com/redirect/e96d190e-af66-4ec5-b937-1eebb6e5b597?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [government](https://substack.com/redirect/ca355246-a95c-45c2-a6a0-3c6047cf5b09?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [inefficiency](https://substack.com/redirect/066eab18-e35c-4121-b3f5-98e179dab98d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), why don't they show us the way: **merge counties and create a better, leaner, more efficient government for everyone**.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTYwMDk5NDUyLCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzQ0MTIxNTU4LCJleHAiOjE3NDY3MTM1NTgsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.-5yxMeG4WCeEoUO0uEsCpxGuhQlbg5cOh5MBRQO1o0g?)

More [in the body of the article above](https://substack.com/redirect/8acbcc07-2186-4d14-9a09-674b619b3008?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but because I was constrained by the data available (since [counties are generally exempt](https://substack.com/redirect/b3ad1dd7-37a0-46ab-9c40-846af0d04ff7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from having to submit annual reports with the State Auditor), I tried to present a smattering of small counties to show how they compare to the state's largest.
* Here's where I pulled data for each of the counties: [Putnam](https://substack.com/redirect/b5854444-f6f5-4319-889b-e5173622bb4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Sullivan](https://substack.com/redirect/4eeec837-d014-44da-9e28-599813edc499?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Clark](https://substack.com/redirect/bcd5f3cf-b861-47c5-924c-153873450e02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Chariton](https://substack.com/redirect/b7f17dd3-2dd1-4dcf-984d-01dd8438735a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Montgomery](https://substack.com/redirect/82be7a60-b3ea-4cef-ab1c-6e9a91fb74ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Linn](https://substack.com/redirect/5c33cafb-4fe6-4bb0-9bf4-912a054a9427?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [McDonald](https://substack.com/redirect/b8542f80-ce79-4215-9a22-2a9dc68decdc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [St. Louis County](https://substack.com/redirect/1dc2bd87-a3f9-4b7b-b172-462b7b090da1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* [St. Louis City](https://substack.com/redirect/ea961210-f3f9-4cb3-951e-d85ca8118252?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [St. Louis County](https://substack.com/redirect/008eafe0-51ed-44b3-98d3-771422a04343?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are separate counties. This is data for St. Louis County and does not include the City. If you're curious, here's a [bit more context on why they're separate](https://substack.com/redirect/0773def3-0aac-4006-ae88-c4c2cd18c1c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Getting really into the weeds: the data for St. Louis County is technically "Expenditures" and not "Disbursements." The two aren't _exactly_ the same, but to use the most fitting possible expression, it's close enough for government work.
* I got per-capita income by Missouri county from the [Missouri Economic Research and Information Center](https://substack.com/redirect/8e815831-9746-4bd1-ad78-37239fddd54a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* I did not include counties where there was something (obviously) anomalous going on. Take [Caldwell County](https://substack.com/redirect/0e881139-dfe9-42aa-bd05-8e1c01fdef33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), for instance--[their costs are ](https://substack.com/redirect/327b8723-4f72-47a4-872b-a202e40ecf26?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[far](https://substack.com/redirect/327b8723-4f72-47a4-872b-a202e40ecf26?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ higher than you'd expect](https://substack.com/redirect/327b8723-4f72-47a4-872b-a202e40ecf26?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for a county of its size, but that's a function of a one-time infrastructure project. (The [Little Otter Creek Reservoir](https://substack.com/redirect/7efa01ac-7483-4fdd-b3e9-9d3a3830d34d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), if you're curious.) However, just about every county spent [ARPA](https://substack.com/redirect/8cc874a1-ee92-44f6-bbfd-c12ac58c9b6c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) money, and while that may be a one-time thing, I didn't exclude that from the analysis.

Finally: can we get more creative with our county names? Across the U.S., there are [nine Putnam Counties](https://substack.com/redirect/c0cf6b32-d63d-4104-a1ab-4e6020adb267?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [six Sullivan Counties](https://substack.com/redirect/28021639-6ec5-4310-98f9-57f595cca29f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [12 Clark Counties](https://substack.com/redirect/d52ac560-2278-4557-a452-b3be6e46c9aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [18 Montgomery Counties](https://substack.com/redirect/5ac172d9-5fa0-47dd-b5f9-7fd26db3a87c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and four [Linn Counties](https://substack.com/redirect/6c2a2e8f-8dee-4cf0-adba-d3b6d9725ac3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). There are even two [St. Louis Counties](https://substack.com/redirect/75fcaaee-85b8-4ecc-8a08-bd65f561581a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
(McDonald County, while unique, still doesn't feel very fun or creative. Credit where credit is due: Chariton County is unique and a good county name.)
Give us a [Musial](https://substack.com/redirect/0413dceb-6ac9-48d0-80bb-3db5fc2c55fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) County! Or a [Ted Drewes](https://substack.com/redirect/f70627b0-d706-4ea7-b75f-486b90a8385e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) County! [Toasted Ravioli](https://substack.com/redirect/a21161fe-4028-4333-a168-bdabb0314971?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) County, Missouri has real potential as a tourist draw. We should be having way more fun with this.
8
Every county has three County Commissioners, an Assessor, a Circuit Clerk, a Collector, a County Clerk, an elected Coroner, a Prosecuting Attorney, a Public Administrator, a Recorder, a Treasurer, and a Sheriff. (Some of the larger counties have a different set of elected officials. But this is the case in most rural counties.)
In some cases, some of those positions are merged (like Collector and Treasurer). But even still: it's a _lot_ of elected officials.
9
Two states with higher rates of rural roads in poor condition are [Rhode Island and Hawaii](https://substack.com/redirect/af733935-1ea1-489d-9c9c-54f5c551f2fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), who have very few rural road miles. In terms of rural road miles in poor condition, I'd venture that Missouri is among the highest.
10
There's more than a little nuance here in what exactly each county is responsible for, between [county-urban road systems](https://substack.com/redirect/00386e51-06b1-4d63-81c7-d369dda091fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [county highway commissions](https://substack.com/redirect/42e60caf-8c57-4c00-aaf7-7a054ce5767c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and a bunch of other things, but what's clear is that counties are generally on the hook for some meaningful amount of road repair and upkeep.
11
Here are the sources for [Putnam](https://substack.com/redirect/b5854444-f6f5-4319-889b-e5173622bb4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Sullivan](https://substack.com/redirect/4eeec837-d014-44da-9e28-599813edc499?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [Clark](https://substack.com/redirect/bcd5f3cf-b861-47c5-924c-153873450e02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Counties, all from 2023.
The [most recent McDonald County financials](https://substack.com/redirect/b8542f80-ce79-4215-9a22-2a9dc68decdc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) don't separate out coroner compensation, which is itself part of the challenge here: it's very hard to do a proper compare/contrast, and it makes accountability really difficult. Instead, I pulled [this data from 2021](https://substack.com/redirect/51b04661-f305-469e-b572-903cb14902d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It's a few years old, and so I'm guessing that his compensation has gone up, but it doesn't change the overall story here.
Then there's a broader question here of why Missouri has elected coroners at all. Even 80 years ago, the state suggested [abolishing the position](https://substack.com/redirect/9a847d2d-8fe1-49e3-95c4-bbb359eba7ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), thinking back then that it may not be necessary. But like the whole infrastructure of counties, it's managed to hang on.
12
Or maybe they're not. I have no idea. Maybe I'll meet them one day.
13
This data is, technically, [how many Americans ](https://substack.com/redirect/56502a5c-73e5-4f88-85ff-9755d9a3b07c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[have](https://substack.com/redirect/56502a5c-73e5-4f88-85ff-9755d9a3b07c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ run for public office](https://substack.com/redirect/56502a5c-73e5-4f88-85ff-9755d9a3b07c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), not how many want to. But this is probably the more important data anyway: speaking from experience, there is a really big chasm between thinking you want to run and actually putting together a campaign, no matter what position you're running for. [It's hard to run for office](https://substack.com/redirect/061acc36-3b83-4f70-bbf0-ad0ac05a5b22?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)!
14
This isn't an attack on the Auditor's Office, by the way. They do good work, often thanklessly. But they [only have 115 employees](https://substack.com/redirect/a8a23f43-edfb-4437-af55-b6f1c67077b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and there's only so much they can do when you think about everything else they have to look at across the state. But the [statute that exempts counties from reporting requirements](https://substack.com/redirect/b3ad1dd7-37a0-46ab-9c40-846af0d04ff7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) makes absolutely no sense.
15
The "North Missouri nonmetropolitan area," technically.
16
Two things:
* This includes sports reporters, meteorologists, etc. So there are even fewer political reporters.
* Alarmingly: since I started working on this post, the data seems to have disappeared from the Bureau of Labor Statistics. (I don't know why that is. [I've complained about government data before](https://substack.com/redirect/ef4e025d-b3f3-4007-81a3-2b5aa6137fe5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but this is the first time I can remember it just disappearing like this. I've sent some texts around and have reason to think this might be a function of DOGE cuts, which is pretty unsettling if that's true. But I don't know that definitively.) Fortunately, the data is still available on the [Wayback Machine](https://substack.com/redirect/4a2229cc-2325-4871-85ef-7f80ed5e4c41?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

17
"[List of former United States counties](https://substack.com/redirect/def8d754-14c6-4da7-8541-4635cdb1865e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" is the Wikipedia page you never knew you needed! But you can use it to see just how seldom anything has changed in the last 125 years.

---
